name = Kushiiwamado Shrine | image = [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tamba-Sasayama|Q877169|WD=Q877169}} Fukui 1170 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha, two seats)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} }} [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kushiiwamado Shrine''' (くしいわまどじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Fukui, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo. It is an Engishiki Meishin-taisha shrine (two seats) and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto}} * Omiyahime-no-Mikoto === About the deities === Kushiiwamado and Toyoiwamado are known in classical texts as gate-guardian deities. In the ''Kojiki,'' they appear as alternate names of Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami, described as "deities of the gate." In ''Kogo Shui,'' they guard the palace gates after Amaterasu's relocation. The Engishiki also lists them among palace gate deities. Their exact historical relation to this shrine remains unclear, though Edo scholarship linked this shrine as their principal cult site. === History === The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records five households of shrine endowment for "Kushiiwamado deity" in Tanba Province, the earliest textual attestation. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), it appears as "Kushiiwamado Shrine, two seats, both Meishin-taisha" in Taki District, Tanba Province. The shrine preserves transmission of three Heian-period deity statues (Important Cultural Properties). In medieval records it appears as "Oimo-sha." After repeated war damage post-Onin War, it was rebuilt in 1567; it was later burned again during warfare in the Tensho era and lost much of its shrine lands. In early modern times, buildings were reconstructed (records exist for 1663 honden work and 1775 haiden work). It was known as "Kushiiwamado Daimyojin" and had a shrine-temple called Omiya-ji. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine; in 1904 it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Precinct === [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] The current honden dates to 1959. A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしいわまとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしいわまとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Kushiiwamado-jinja}} [[JA:櫛石窓神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17218980}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kushiiwamado Shrine | image = [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tamba-Sasayama|Q877169|WD=Q877169|1=Tamba-Sasayama}} Fukui 1170 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha, two seats)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} }} [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kushiiwamado Shrine''' (くしいわまどじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Fukui, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo. It is an Engishiki Meishin-taisha shrine (two seats) and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * Omiyahime-no-Mikoto === About the deities === Kushiiwamado and Toyoiwamado are known in classical texts as gate-guardian deities. In the ''Kojiki,'' they appear as alternate names of Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami, described as "deities of the gate." In ''Kogo Shui,'' they guard the palace gates after Amaterasu's relocation. The Engishiki also lists them among palace gate deities. Their exact historical relation to this shrine remains unclear, though Edo scholarship linked this shrine as their principal cult site. === History === The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records five households of shrine endowment for "Kushiiwamado deity" in Tanba Province, the earliest textual attestation. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), it appears as "Kushiiwamado Shrine, two seats, both Meishin-taisha" in Taki District, Tanba Province. The shrine preserves transmission of three Heian-period deity statues (Important Cultural Properties). In medieval records it appears as "Oimo-sha." After repeated war damage post-Onin War, it was rebuilt in 1567; it was later burned again during warfare in the Tensho era and lost much of its shrine lands. In early modern times, buildings were reconstructed (records exist for 1663 honden work and 1775 haiden work). It was known as "Kushiiwamado Daimyojin" and had a shrine-temple called Omiya-ji. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine; in 1904 it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Precinct === [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] The current honden dates to 1959. A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしいわまとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしいわまとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

category|Category:Kushiiwamado-jinja}} [[JA:櫛石窓神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17218980}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kushiiwamado Shrine | image = [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tamba-Sasayama|Q877169|WD=Q877169|1=Tamba-Sasayama}} Fukui 1170 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha, two seats)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} }} [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kushiiwamado Shrine''' (くしいわまどじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Fukui, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo. It is an Engishiki Meishin-taisha shrine (two seats) and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * Omiyahime-no-Mikoto === About the deities === Kushiiwamado and Toyoiwamado are known in classical texts as gate-guardian deities. In the ''Kojiki,'' they appear as alternate names of Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami, described as "deities of the gate." In ''Kogo Shui,'' they guard the palace gates after Amaterasu's relocation. The Engishiki also lists them among palace gate deities. Their exact historical relation to this shrine remains unclear, though Edo scholarship linked this shrine as their principal cult site. === History === The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records five households of shrine endowment for "Kushiiwamado deity" in Tanba Province, the earliest textual attestation. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), it appears as "Kushiiwamado Shrine, two seats, both Meishin-taisha" in Taki District, Tanba Province. The shrine preserves transmission of three Heian-period deity statues (Important Cultural Properties). In medieval records it appears as "Oimo-sha." After repeated war damage post-Onin War, it was rebuilt in 1567; it was later burned again during warfare in the Tensho era and lost much of its shrine lands. In early modern times, buildings were reconstructed (records exist for 1663 honden work and 1775 haiden work). It was known as "Kushiiwamado Daimyojin" and had a shrine-temple called Omiya-ji. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine; in 1904 it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Precinct === [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] The current honden dates to 1959. A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

category|Category:Kushiiwamado-jinja}} [[JA:櫛石窓神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17218980}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kushiiwamado Shrine | image = [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tamba-Sasayama|Q877169|WD=Q877169|1=Tamba-Sasayama}} Fukui 1170 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha, two seats)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} }} [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kushiiwamado Shrine''' (くしいわまどじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Fukui, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo. It is an Engishiki Meishin-taisha shrine (two seats) and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * Omiyahime-no-Mikoto === About the deities === Kushiiwamado and Toyoiwamado are known in classical texts as gate-guardian deities. In the ''Kojiki,'' they appear as alternate names of Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami, described as "deities of the gate." In ''Kogo Shui,'' they guard the palace gates after Amaterasu's relocation. The Engishiki also lists them among palace gate deities. Their exact historical relation to this shrine remains unclear, though Edo scholarship linked this shrine as their principal cult site. === History === The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records five households of shrine endowment for "Kushiiwamado deity" in Tanba Province, the earliest textual attestation. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), it appears as "Kushiiwamado Shrine, two seats, both Meishin-taisha" in Taki District, Tanba Province. The shrine preserves transmission of three Heian-period deity statues (Important Cultural Properties). In medieval records it appears as "Oimo-sha." After repeated war damage post-Onin War, it was rebuilt in 1567; it was later burned again during warfare in the Tensho era and lost much of its shrine lands. In early modern times, buildings were reconstructed (records exist for 1663 honden work and 1775 haiden work). It was known as "Kushiiwamado Daimyojin" and had a shrine-temple called Omiya-ji. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine; in 1904 it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Precinct === [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] The current honden dates to 1959. A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

category|Category:Kushiiwamado-jinja}} [[JA:櫛石窓神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17218980}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kushiiwamado Shrine | image = [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tamba-Sasayama|Q877169|WD=Q877169|1=Tamba-Sasayama}} Fukui 1170 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha, two seats)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} }} [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kushiiwamado Shrine''' (くしいわまどじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Fukui, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo. It is an Engishiki Meishin-taisha shrine (two seats) and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * Omiyahime-no-Mikoto === About the deities === Kushiiwamado and Toyoiwamado are known in classical texts as gate-guardian deities. In the ''Kojiki,'' they appear as alternate names of Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami, described as "deities of the gate." In ''Kogo Shui,'' they guard the palace gates after Amaterasu's relocation. The Engishiki also lists them among palace gate deities. Their exact historical relation to this shrine remains unclear, though Edo scholarship linked this shrine as their principal cult site. === History === The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records five households of shrine endowment for "Kushiiwamado deity" in Tanba Province, the earliest textual attestation. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), it appears as "Kushiiwamado Shrine, two seats, both Meishin-taisha" in Taki District, Tanba Province. The shrine preserves transmission of three Heian-period deity statues (Important Cultural Properties). In medieval records it appears as "Oimo-sha." After repeated war damage post-Onin War, it was rebuilt in 1567; it was later burned again during warfare in the Tensho era and lost much of its shrine lands. In early modern times, buildings were reconstructed (records exist for 1663 honden work and 1775 haiden work). It was known as "Kushiiwamado Daimyojin" and had a shrine-temple called Omiya-ji. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine; in 1904 it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Precinct === [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] The current honden dates to 1959. A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

category|Category:Kushiiwamado-jinja}} [[JA:櫛石窓神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17218980}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kushiiwamado Shrine | image = [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tamba-Sasayama|Q877169|WD=Q877169|1=Tamba-Sasayama}} Fukui 1170 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha, two seats)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} }} [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kushiiwamado Shrine''' (くしいわまどじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Fukui, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo. It is an Engishiki Meishin-taisha shrine (two seats) and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * Omiyahime-no-Mikoto === About the deities === Kushiiwamado and Toyoiwamado are known in classical texts as gate-guardian deities. In the ''Kojiki,'' they appear as alternate names of Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami, described as "deities of the gate." In ''Kogo Shui,'' they guard the palace gates after Amaterasu's relocation. The Engishiki also lists them among palace gate deities. Their exact historical relation to this shrine remains unclear, though Edo scholarship linked this shrine as their principal cult site. === History === The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records five households of shrine endowment for "Kushiiwamado deity" in Tanba Province, the earliest textual attestation. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), it appears as "Kushiiwamado Shrine, two seats, both Meishin-taisha" in Taki District, Tanba Province. The shrine preserves transmission of three Heian-period deity statues (Important Cultural Properties). In medieval records it appears as "Oimo-sha." After repeated war damage post-Onin War, it was rebuilt in 1567; it was later burned again during warfare in the Tensho era and lost much of its shrine lands. In early modern times, buildings were reconstructed (records exist for 1663 honden work and 1775 haiden work). It was known as "Kushiiwamado Daimyojin" and had a shrine-temple called Omiya-ji. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine; in 1904 it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Precinct === [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] The current honden dates to 1959. A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]]

category|Category:Kushiiwamado-jinja}} [[JA:櫛石窓神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17218980}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kushiiwamado Shrine | image = [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tamba-Sasayama|Q877169|qid=Q877169|1=Tamba-Sasayama}} Fukui 1170 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha, two seats)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|qid=Q2535|1=April 29}} }} [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kushiiwamado Shrine''' (くしいわまどじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Fukui, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo. It is an Engishiki Meishin-taisha shrine (two seats) and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * Omiyahime-no-Mikoto === About the deities === Kushiiwamado and Toyoiwamado are known in classical texts as gate-guardian deities. In the ''Kojiki,'' they appear as alternate names of Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami, described as "deities of the gate." In ''Kogo Shui,'' they guard the palace gates after Amaterasu's relocation. The Engishiki also lists them among palace gate deities. Their exact historical relation to this shrine remains unclear, though Edo scholarship linked this shrine as their principal cult site. === History === The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records five households of shrine endowment for "Kushiiwamado deity" in Tanba Province, the earliest textual attestation. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), it appears as "Kushiiwamado Shrine, two seats, both Meishin-taisha" in Taki District, Tanba Province. The shrine preserves transmission of three Heian-period deity statues (Important Cultural Properties). In medieval records it appears as "Oimo-sha." After repeated war damage post-Onin War, it was rebuilt in 1567; it was later burned again during warfare in the Tensho era and lost much of its shrine lands. In early modern times, buildings were reconstructed (records exist for 1663 honden work and 1775 haiden work). It was known as "Kushiiwamado Daimyojin" and had a shrine-temple called Omiya-ji. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine; in 1904 it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Precinct === [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] The current honden dates to 1959. A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]]

A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]]

category|Category:Kushiiwamado-jinja}} [[JA:櫛石窓神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17218980}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kushiiwamado Shrine | image = [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tamba-Sasayama|Q877169|qid=Q877169|1=Tamba-Sasayama}} Fukui 1170 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha, two seats)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|qid=Q2535|1=April 29}} }} [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kushiiwamado Shrine''' (くしいわまどじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Fukui, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo. It is an Engishiki Meishin-taisha shrine (two seats) and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Kushiiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * Omiyahime-no-Mikoto === About the deities === Kushiiwamado and Toyoiwamado are known in classical texts as gate-guardian deities. In the ''Kojiki,'' they appear as alternate names of Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami, described as "deities of the gate." In ''Kogo Shui,'' they guard the palace gates after Amaterasu's relocation. The Engishiki also lists them among palace gate deities. Their exact historical relation to this shrine remains unclear, though Edo scholarship linked this shrine as their principal cult site. === History === The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records five households of shrine endowment for "Kushiiwamado deity" in Tanba Province, the earliest textual attestation. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), it appears as "Kushiiwamado Shrine, two seats, both Meishin-taisha" in Taki District, Tanba Province. The shrine preserves transmission of three Heian-period deity statues (Important Cultural Properties). In medieval records it appears as "Oimo-sha." After repeated war damage post-Onin War, it was rebuilt in 1567; it was later burned again during warfare in the Tensho era and lost much of its shrine lands. In early modern times, buildings were reconstructed (records exist for 1663 honden work and 1775 haiden work). It was known as "Kushiiwamado Daimyojin" and had a shrine-temple called Omiya-ji. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine; in 1904 it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Precinct === [[File:Kushiiwamado-jinja honden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] The current honden dates to 1959. A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]]

A sacred spring once called "Fukui" stood near the shrine and is said to be the origin of the place-name. Behind the shrine is a hill called Miyayama with a sacred rock (iwakura), regarded as a remnant of ancient ritual practice. File:Kushiiwamado-jinja Fukui.jpg|Monument of former sacred spring site File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-2.jpg|Miyayama hill and shrine grove File:Kushiiwamado-jinja miyayama-3.jpg|View toward summit sacred rock === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari Shrine * Fukui Village Local Deity Hall * Karai Daimyojin === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Wooden seated statue of Kushi-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Toyo-Iwado deity * Wooden seated statue of Omiyahime deity (All from the Heian period; designated April 17, 1911) The first two statues were stolen sometime between December 1981 and April 1982. === Site information === * Address: Fukui 1170, Tamba-Sasayama, Hyogo * Access: Wing Shinki bus to "Tamba Omiya" stop, then immediately nearby === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' 29-1: Hyogo place names * ''Nihon no Kamigami'' 7: San'in === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato-wake-no-kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kushiiwamado-jinja}} * Hyogo Association of Shinto Shrines page * Kokugakuin source compilation page {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushi iwamado Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province|名くしいわまとしんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:丹波篠山市の建築物]]
